title: Tenant refusing to vacate house in Delhi? Key legal provisions for eviction and rights of landlords explained
summary: If a tenant refuses to vacate a house in Delhi, there are key provisions under the Delhi Rent Control Act that can protect the landlord's rights. They include eviction, legal remedies, and Supreme Court rulings for landlords and tenants.

title: SCSS maturity rules explained: Can senior citizens extend their account's tenure?
summary: The Senior Citizens Savings Scheme offers retirees a popular fixed-income investment with government backing and quarterly payouts. Eligible individuals can invest, extend their accounts multiple times, and earn an 8.2% annual interest rate.&nbsp;

title: Gen Z investors are rewriting the rules of investing
summary: Gen Z has become the country's fastest-growing investor cohort, with under-30s now comprising 38% of the National Stock Exchange's base, but experts warn their decisions are driven more by social media, peer pressure and post-covid return euphoria than by sound financial strategy.

title: I asked ChatGPT: Can I do a solo Bali trip from Delhi in ₹1 lakh? AI reveals the real 2026 cost

title: Missed an EMI payment? Here are 5 ways default can impact your credit score
summary: EMI bounce on any form of debt can quickly damage your credit score. A single missed payment may significantly reduce scores, affect loans, and increase interest rates for borrowers over time.&nbsp;

title: Can low-volatility funds work amid market volatility?
summary: These passive funds are falling less than the broader market during sharp corrections, but advisers say the trade-off is slower gains in strong rallies.

title: Why sideways markets make even smart investors look confused
summary: After the 2020–24 bull run, markets have turned sideways and volatile. In this phase, discipline, risk control and defensive positioning matter more than aggressive stock picking.

title: Buyback alert! Onward Tech announces Rs 180 crore share buyback at 20% premium
summary: Onward Technologies has approved a Rs 180 crore share buyback, repurchasing nearly 5.49 lakh shares at Rs 328 apiece, a 20% premium. Promoters will not participate in this first-ever buyback, with May 18 set as the record date. The company appointed Centrum Broking as the buyback manager.
